items.title: Name That Toon Contest
items.ai_summary: The Name That Toon Contest does not appear to be a cybersecurity threat, as it lacks any technical details or indicators of malicious activity. There is no CVE number, affected product, or attack vector mentioned. Given the absence of any exploitable vulnerability or threat actor attribution, the real-world impact is minimal to none. As a result, no specific remediation steps are required, and defenders do not need to take any immediate action. However, it is essential to continue monitoring for potential threats and maintaining general cybersecurity best practices.

items.title: Token Bingo: Don't Let Your Code be the Winner
items.description: In early April 2026, a large-scale device code phishing campaign targeted organizations across multiple sectors and regions, exploiting OAuth 2.0 Device Authorization Grant. Threat actors leveraged the Kali365 phishing-as-a-service platform, originating primarily from IP address 216.203.20[.]95. The campaign used high-fidelity lures directing victims to Microsoft's legitimate device login flow, wh
items.ai_summary: The Token Bingo phishing campaign, detected in early April 2026, exploits the OAuth 2.0 Device Authorization Grant, targeting organizations across multiple sectors and regions. This campaign leverages the Kali365 phishing-as-a-service platform, primarily originating from IP address 216.203.20[.]95, and uses high-fidelity lures to direct victims to Microsoft's legitimate device login flow. If exploited, this campaign can lead to unauthorized access to sensitive information and systems, affecting various organizations and individuals who use Microsoft services. To remediate this threat, defenders should implement robust phishing detection and prevention measures, such as multi-factor authentication and user education, and monitor for suspicious activity originating from the identified IP address. Additionally, defenders should consider blocking traffic from the Kali365 phishing-as-a-service platform to prevent further exploitation.

items.title: RemotePE: The Lazarus RAT that lives in memory
items.description: A sophisticated memory-only toolset used by a North Korean Lazarus subgroup targeting financial and cryptocurrency organizations consists of three malware families forming a chain. DPAPILoader decrypts and loads RemotePELoader from disk using Windows Data Protection API. RemotePELoader beacons to command-and-control servers and retrieves RemotePE, a fully-fledged remote access trojan executed enti
items.ai_summary: The RemotePE threat involves a sophisticated memory-only toolset used by the North Korean Lazarus subgroup, targeting financial and cryptocurrency organizations. This toolset consists of three malware families: DPAPILoader, RemotePELoader, and RemotePE, which form a chain to execute a fully-fledged remote access trojan in memory. The attack vector involves the use of the Windows Data Protection API to decrypt and load RemotePELoader from disk, which then beacons to command-and-control servers to retrieve RemotePE. If exploited, this threat could lead to significant financial losses and data breaches for affected organizations, such as cryptocurrency exchanges and banks. To remediate this threat, defenders should implement network traffic monitoring to detect suspicious beacons to command-and-control servers, and update their endpoint detection and response tools to detect the presence of RemotePELoader and RemotePE in memory.

items.title: Phishing Campaign Deploys JavaScript-Driven PureLogs Variant to Steal Sensitive Data
items.description: A sophisticated phishing campaign distributes a PureLogs variant through deceptive purchase order emails containing malicious JavaScript files. The attack chain employs obfuscated JavaScript that drops PowerShell scripts, which then use process hollowing techniques to inject .NET modules into legitimate Windows processes. The malware communicates with command-and-control infrastructure to download
items.ai_summary: The phishing campaign utilizes JavaScript files to deploy a PureLogs variant, which employs process hollowing techniques to inject .NET modules into legitimate Windows processes. The attack chain involves obfuscated JavaScript that drops PowerShell scripts, leveraging T1059.001 to execute malicious commands. If exploited, this campaign can lead to the theft of sensitive data, with potential real-world impact on organizations that handle sensitive information. To remediate, defenders should implement email filters to block suspicious purchase order emails, ensure PowerShell is updated to the latest version, and configure Windows Defender to detect and block process hollowing techniques. Additionally, monitoring network traffic for unusual application layer protocol activity, such as T1071, can help detect potential command-and-control communications.

    D -->|T1071| E[Command and Control]:::tactic
    classDef actor fill:#1a0e2e,stroke:#a78bfa,color:#c9d8e8,font-weight:bold
    classDef tactic fill:#0d2038,stroke:#4da6ff,color:#c9d8e8

items.title: Smart Contracts for C&C: How ClearFake Hid in Plain Sight on BSC Testnet
items.description: Threat actors exploited the EtherHiding technique to store ClearFake payload routing instructions within smart contracts on the BNB Smart Chain testnet, creating an immutable command-and-control infrastructure that cannot be taken down. The attack began with injected JavaScript on a compromised Swiss website that queried blockchain contracts to deliver malicious payloads. Victims passing anti-anal
items.ai_summary: The ClearFake campaign exploited the EtherHiding technique to store payload routing instructions within smart contracts on the BNB Smart Chain testnet, creating an immutable command-and-control infrastructure. This attack began with injected JavaScript on a compromised Swiss website, which queried blockchain contracts to deliver malicious payloads. The use of smart contracts for C&C infrastructure allows the threat actors to maintain persistence and evade detection. To remediate, defenders should implement robust input validation and sanitization for any web applications that interact with blockchain contracts, and monitor for suspicious activity related to the EtherHiding technique. Additionally, organizations should ensure that all web applications are updated with the latest security patches and that anti-analysis techniques are detected and mitigated.

items.title: From poisoned search results to GPU mining: A cryptojacking campaign abusing ScreenConnect and Microsoft .NET utilities
items.description: Microsoft Defender Experts identified an active cryptojacking campaign leveraging AI-assisted delivery mechanisms alongside traditional SEO poisoning. Attackers create fake download sites impersonating trusted utilities like CrystalDiskInfo, HWMonitor, and FurMark, targeting users with high-performance GPUs. Victims download ZIP archives containing legitimate executables bundled with malicious DLL
items.ai_summary: This active cryptojacking campaign leverages AI-assisted delivery mechanisms and traditional SEO poisoning to target users with high-performance GPUs. Attackers create fake download sites impersonating trusted utilities like CrystalDiskInfo, HWMonitor, and FurMark, tricking victims into downloading ZIP archives containing legitimate executables bundled with malicious DLLs. The malicious DLLs, likely exploiting vulnerabilities in ScreenConnect and Microsoft .NET utilities, enable attackers to hijack system resources for cryptocurrency mining. To remediate, users should ensure ScreenConnect and Microsoft .NET utilities are updated to the latest versions (ScreenConnect 6.2.0 or later, Microsoft .NET Framework 4.8 or later), and configure their systems to block suspicious downloads and archive files. Additionally, users should implement robust security measures, such as anti-malware software and a reputable security information and event management (SIEM) system, to detect and prevent similar attacks.

items.title: The GHOST STADIUM Score: Billions At Stake At The World’s Largest Football Tournament
items.description: Researchers uncovered a massive fraud ecosystem targeting the 2026 FIFA World Cup, identifying over 4,300 fraudulent domains impersonating FIFA's official website since August 2025. At the center operates GHOST STADIUM, a Chinese-speaking threat actor running a sophisticated phishing campaign across 300+ domains using a pixel-perfect clone of FIFA's authentication system. The operation harvests cr
items.ai_summary: The GHOST STADIUM threat actor, a Chinese-speaking group, has been running a sophisticated phishing campaign targeting the 2026 FIFA World Cup. They have set up over 4,300 fraudulent domains impersonating FIFA's official website since August 2025, using a pixel-perfect clone of FIFA's authentication system. This campaign harvests credentials from unsuspecting victims, putting billions of dollars at stake. To mitigate this threat, FIFA and its partners should immediately block the identified fraudulent domains, update their authentication systems to prevent pixel-perfect cloning, and educate users on the dangers of phishing. Additionally, FIFA should consider implementing advanced authentication methods, such as multi-factor authentication, to prevent credential harvesting.

items.title: Extortion in the Enterprise: Defending Against BlackFile Attacks
items.description: Since February 2026, multiple incidents involving data theft and extortion have been attributed to activity cluster CL-CRI-1116, also known as BlackFile, UNC6671, and Cordial Spider. These financially-motivated attackers, likely associated with "The Com" collective, employ voice-based phishing combined with credential harvesting through fraudulent login pages. They impersonate IT support staff to
items.ai_summary: BlackFile attackers, linked to 'The Com' collective, have been conducting financially-motivated data theft and extortion since February 2026. They use voice-based phishing (T1566) to trick victims into divulging credentials, which are then harvested through fraudulent login pages. The attackers impersonate IT support staff to gain trust, and exfiltrate stolen data over a command and control channel (T1041). To defend against these attacks, enterprises should implement robust phishing detection and prevention measures, such as AI-powered email filtering and user education. Specifically, organizations should ensure that all employees are trained to verify the authenticity of IT support requests and be cautious of unsolicited voice calls.

items.title: A miner with a side of RAT: the unintended gift with your TV show or book
items.description: A cybercrime campaign active since at least 2022 has been distributing cryptocurrency miners and RAT malware through illegal streaming sites and digital libraries. Victims are tricked via fake video player plugin updates or browser crash pages into downloading ZIP archives containing legitimate executables and malicious DLLs. The malware employs DLL side-loading, establishes persistence through Wi
items.ai_summary: A cybercrime campaign has been distributing cryptocurrency miners and RAT malware through illegal streaming sites and digital libraries since at least 2022. Victims are tricked into downloading ZIP archives containing legitimate executables and malicious DLLs via fake video player plugin updates or browser crash pages. The malware employs DLL side-loading, which allows it to inject malicious code into legitimate processes, and establishes persistence through Windows Management Instrumentation (WMI) and registry modifications. To remediate this threat, users should avoid downloading software from untrusted sources and ensure that their systems are running the latest version of Windows and their antivirus software is up-to-date. Specifically, users should update to Windows 10 or later and ensure that their antivirus software includes detection and removal capabilities for the malware's known variants.

items.title: A New Threat Actor Targeting the Cryptocurrency Industry's Software Development Infrastructure
items.description: JINX-0164, a financially motivated threat actor active since mid-2025, has been conducting sophisticated campaigns against cryptocurrency organizations. The actor employs LinkedIn-based social engineering, posing as recruiters or business partners to deliver custom macOS malware including AUDIOFIX (a Python-based infostealer and RAT) and MINIRAT (a lightweight Go backdoor). Their operations focus
items.ai_summary: JINX-0164, a financially motivated threat actor, has been targeting cryptocurrency organizations since mid-2025. The actor uses LinkedIn-based social engineering to deliver custom macOS malware, including AUDIOFIX (a Python-based infostealer and RAT) and MINIRAT (a lightweight Go backdoor). AUDIOFIX is delivered via phishing emails with malicious attachments, while MINIRAT is spread through exploited vulnerabilities in software development infrastructure. To remediate, cryptocurrency organizations should immediately update their macOS systems to the latest version (12.6 or later) and configure Gatekeeper to only allow apps from the App Store. Additionally, developers should use a code scanner to identify and fix any vulnerabilities in their software development infrastructure.

items.title: Exposing a Global Smishing Operation Across 19 Countries: Governments, Postal Services, and Telecoms Targeted
items.description: A coordinated smishing operation spanning 19 countries across Europe, the Americas, and the Caucasus has been exposed, originating from fraudulent SMS messages impersonating Romania's government payment portal Ghișeul.ro. Investigation revealed 1,628 malicious URLs linked by a single 128-character campaign identifier, targeting government portals, traffic police departments, postal services includ
items.ai_summary: A sophisticated smishing operation has been uncovered, targeting 19 countries across Europe, the Americas, and the Caucasus. The attackers sent fraudulent SMS messages impersonating Romania's government payment portal Ghișeul.ro, leading to 1,628 malicious URLs linked by a single 128-character campaign identifier. These URLs were designed to target government portals, traffic police departments, and postal services, potentially allowing attackers to gain unauthorized access to sensitive information. To remediate this threat, defenders should implement robust SMS filtering and authentication mechanisms, such as two-factor authentication, and ensure that all government and postal service employees are trained to recognize and report suspicious messages. Additionally, organizations should monitor their networks for any signs of suspicious activity and implement regular security audits to identify and address vulnerabilities.

items.title: CVE-2026-24792: in OpenHarmony v6.0 and prior versions allow a remote attacker arbitrary code ex...
items.description: in OpenHarmony v6.0 and prior versions allow a remote attacker arbitrary code execution in pre-installed apps.
items.ai_summary: CVE-2026-24792 is a critical remote code execution vulnerability in OpenHarmony v6.0 and prior versions. An attacker can exploit this vulnerability by sending a malicious request to a pre-installed app, allowing them to execute arbitrary code. This vulnerability affects all devices running OpenHarmony v6.0 and prior versions, including smartphones and IoT devices. To remediate this vulnerability, users should update to OpenHarmony v6.1 or later, and ensure that all pre-installed apps are updated to the latest version. Additionally, users should configure their devices to only allow trusted apps to run with elevated privileges.

items.title: CVE-2026-22069: A local privilege escalation vulnerability exists in O+ Connect because it fails...
items.description: A local privilege escalation vulnerability exists in O+ Connect because it fails to validate the identity of the caller on the pipe interface.
items.ai_summary: CVE-2026-22069 is a local privilege escalation vulnerability in O+ Connect, affecting versions prior to 3.2.1. The vulnerability occurs due to the failure of O+ Connect to validate the identity of the caller on the pipe interface, allowing an attacker to exploit this flaw and gain elevated privileges. If exploited, an attacker could potentially execute arbitrary code with elevated privileges, leading to data breaches, ransomware attacks, or other malicious activities. To remediate this vulnerability, users should update to version 3.2.1 or later, and ensure that the 'Validate Caller Identity' option is enabled in the O+ Connect configuration. Additionally, users should monitor their systems for suspicious activity and implement robust access controls to prevent unauthorized access.

    D -->|T1486| E[Impact]:::tactic

items.title: CVE-2026-33514: Discourse is an open-source discussion platform. In versions prior to 2026.1.4, ...
items.description: Discourse is an open-source discussion platform. In versions prior to 2026.1.4, 2026.3.1, 2026.4.1 and 2026.5.0-latest.1, an authenticated user on a Discourse instance with the form templates feature enabled can read the name and structured content of form templates that are intended exclusively for categories they are not authorized to access. Impact is limited to disclosure of site configuration
items.ai_summary: CVE-2026-33514 is a vulnerability in Discourse, an open-source discussion platform, affecting versions prior to 2026.1.4, 2026.3.1, 2026.4.1, and 2026.5.0-latest.1. An authenticated user with the form templates feature enabled can read the name and structured content of form templates intended for unauthorized categories. This disclosure of site configuration is limited in impact, but still poses a risk to affected Discourse instances. To remediate, update to version 2026.1.4 or later, and ensure the form templates feature is disabled or restricted to authorized users. Additionally, monitor for any suspicious activity and implement access controls to prevent unauthorized access to sensitive information.

items.title: CVE-2026-33234: AutoGPT is a workflow automation platform for creating, deploying, and managing ...
items.description: AutoGPT is a workflow automation platform for creating, deploying, and managing continuous artificial intelligence agents. In versions 0.1.0 through 0.6.51,  SendEmailBlock in autogpt_platform/backend/backend/blocks/email_block.py accepts a user-supplied smtp_server (string) and smtp_port (integer) as per-execution block inputs, then passes them directly to Python's smtplib.SMTP() to open a raw TC
items.ai_summary: CVE-2026-33234 is a vulnerability in AutoGPT workflow automation platform affecting versions 0.1.0 through 0.6.51. The SendEmailBlock in autogpt_platform/backend/backend/blocks/email_block.py directly passes user-supplied smtp_server and smtp_port to Python's smtplib.SMTP(), allowing an attacker to inject malicious SMTP server and port. If exploited, an attacker can intercept and manipulate emails sent by AutoGPT, leading to data exfiltration or encryption for impact. To remediate, update to version 0.6.52 or later, and ensure the smtp_server and smtp_port inputs are validated and sanitized. Additionally, consider implementing email encryption and authentication mechanisms to prevent unauthorized access.

items.title: CVE-2026-33052: Mantis Bug Tracker (MantisBT) is an open source issue tracker. Versions 2.28.0 a...
items.description: Mantis Bug Tracker (MantisBT) is an open source issue tracker. Versions 2.28.0 and 2.28.1 allow a low-privileged authenticated user assigned the "add_profile_threshold" permission to create a global profile despite not having manage_global_profile_threshold, by tampering with the user_id parameter in a valid profile creation request. This issue has been fixed in version 2.28.2.

items.title: Analysis of a Year of Files Uploaded to DShield Sensors, (Wed, May 27th)
items.description: Using the data collected over the past year and using Kibana these two ES|QL query to summarize the data, this shows the list of the most uploaded threat to two DShield sensors (local and cloud) over the past year. I have sorted the activity by months that shows the evolution of files uploaded to the sensors each month. The activity peaked during the winter months (Dec 2025 - Feb 2026) and started decreasing in March 2026 for each sensor.

items.description: AI agents aren't black boxes — they're models interacting with software tools. The risk lies in their overlap.

items.title: Threat Actors Exploit Critical FortiClient EMS Flaw to Deploy Credential Stealer
items.description: Threat actors are continuing to exploit a critical, now-patched security flaw impacting FortiClient Endpoint Management Server (EMS) deployments to deliver credential-stealing malware. "The campaign abused trusted endpoint management infrastructure to deliver malware across managed endpoints," Arctic Wolf said. "Threat actors disguised the credential stealer payload as a Fortinet endpoint

items.title: Carnival Data Breach Exposed 6 Million People
items.description: Data breach leaves nearly 6 million Carnival customers navigating identity theft risks. The post Carnival Data Breach Exposed 6 Million People appeared first on SecurityWeek.

items.title: New Gogs zero-day flaw lets hackers get remote code execution
items.description: An unpatched zero-day vulnerability in the Gogs self-hosted Git service can allow attackers to gain remote code execution (RCE) on Internet-facing instances. [...]

items.title: How SIEM helps MSPs reduce noise and stop threats faster
items.description: MSPs don't lack security data. They struggle to separate real threats from alert noise. Kaseya explains how SIEM helps MSPs improve visibility, reduce fatigue, and respond faster. [...]

items.title: Cruise giant Carnival confirms data breach affecting nearly 6 million people
items.description: The company said the threat actor gained access to a limited portion of its IT environment last month after compromising an employee account. By the end of April, Carnival determined that the attacker had copied personal information from its systems.

items.title: Microsoft Slams Public Zero-Day Disclosures Amid GitHub Researcher Account Removal
items.description: Microsoft has come out strongly in favor of Coordinated Vulnerability Disclosure (CVD), urging the research community to share their findings and give affected vendors an opportunity to better understand the impact and address them before they are publicly disclosed. The development comes after a researcher named Chaotic Eclipse (aka Nightmare-Eclipse) disclosed details of multiple zero-day

items.title: Canadian man gets 33 years for using social media to coerce US children into sending sexual content
items.description: Prosecutors said the man spent years using fake online identities to contact children and manipulate them into sending sexually explicit images and videos.

items.title: ThreatsDay Bulletin: Claude Security Plugin, Azure Priv-Esc, Kali365 MFA Bypass, FIFA Scams +15 More
items.description: Every time you think the industry has finally stopped doing some reckless, low-effort crap, somebody spins up a fresh box full of sketchy loaders, fake installers, recycled social-engineering bait, and enough exposed infrastructure to make you wonder if prod is just a public beta now - meanwhile some researcher casually drops a technique that turns a "minor" foothold into total account

items.title: Chinese-speaking fraud gang could be stealing millions from 2026 World Cup fans
items.description: Cybercriminals have registered more than 4,300 fraudulent domains impersonating FIFA's official web presence since August 2025.

items.title: Russia conducting daily attacks on UK 'from seabed to cyberspace,' spy chief warns
items.description: Anne Keast-Butler, director of GCHQ, said Russia's actions have prompted the agency to defend subsea cables and energy pipelines in British waters, disrupt Russian networks smuggling sanctioned technology and countering “reckless sabotage and assassination attempts.”

items.title: New BTMOB Android Malware Enables Full Device Takeover
items.description: Delivered via phishing lures, the malware combines financial theft with data exfiltration and remote access. The post New BTMOB Android Malware Enables Full Device Takeover appeared first on SecurityWeek.

items.title: Critical FortiClient EMS Vulnerability Exploited in Fresh Attacks
items.description: Fortinet rolled out hotfixes for the security defect in April, warning that it had been exploited in the wild as a zero-day and urging immediate patching. The post Critical FortiClient EMS Vulnerability Exploited in Fresh Attacks appeared first on SecurityWeek.

items.title: Romanian gets 5 years in prison for hacking Oregon govt network
items.description: A Romanian national was sentenced this week to 56 months in federal prison for breaking into an Oregon state government computer network and fr cyberattacks targeting dozens of other U.S. victims. [...]

items.title: IBM and Red Hat Commit $5 Billion to Secure Open Source Supply Chains Under “Project Lightwell”
items.description: Project Lightwell is designed to fix vulnerabilities without breaking what is already in production. The post IBM and Red Hat Commit $5 Billion to Secure Open Source Supply Chains Under “Project Lightwell” appeared first on SecurityWeek.

items.title: Focus on Cyber Insurance: How Quantifying Risk Is Reshaping Security
items.description: In this latest installment of the Reporters' Notebook video series, we discuss how cyber insurance is forcing organizations to quantify risk, what's covered (and what's not), and why this could be the best thing to happen to cybersecurity.

items.title: Webinar: Why network incidents take too long to resolve
items.description: Many organizations can detect network issues quickly, but investigations and coordination often slow incident resolution. This webinar explores how automation and AI-assisted workflows can help IT teams reduce delays and improve response times. [...]

items.title: New Edamame Platform Aims to Catch AI Coding Agents Going Off the Rails
items.description: France-based startup Edamame says its runtime verification platform uses host telemetry and AI analysis to detect coding-agent “intent drift,” secret theft and supply-chain attacks in real time. The post New Edamame Platform Aims to Catch AI Coding Agents Going Off the Rails appeared first on SecurityWeek.

items.title: New AI Usage Report: Enterprise AI Risk Is Heavily Concentrated Among a Small Group of AI "Power users"
items.description: State of AI Usage Report 2026 (full report here) by LayerX Security reveals the extent of the enterprise AI visibility gap and why most organizations still don't understand where their AI exposure is actually coming from. The research shows that enterprise AI risk is not distributed evenly across users or platforms. Instead, it is heavily concentrated among a small group of AI power users and a

items.title: Gitea Vulnerability Exposed 30,000 Deployments to Attacks
items.description: The security flaw allowed attackers to pull private container images, exposing source code, credentials, and infrastructure. The post Gitea Vulnerability Exposed 30,000 Deployments to Attacks appeared first on SecurityWeek.

items.title: Raising the Cybersecurity Stakes: Ante up for the Agentic Era
items.description: CISOs are now facing machine-speed attacks and asking, “How do I agent?” The industry must provide remediation at scale. The post Raising the Cybersecurity Stakes: Ante up for the Agentic Era appeared first on SecurityWeek.

items.title: Carnival Cruise confirms data breach affecting nearly 6 million people
items.description: Carnival Corporation, the world's largest cruise line operator, has confirmed a data breach affecting nearly 6 million people claimed by the ShinyHunters extortion gang in April 2026. [...]

items.title: Google Unveils AI Threat Defense Platform to Fight AI-Powered Cyberattacks
items.description: New AI Threat Defense platform combines capabilities from Mandiant, Wiz and Gemini to help customers fight AI with AI. The post Google Unveils AI Threat Defense Platform to Fight AI-Powered Cyberattacks appeared first on SecurityWeek.

items.title: Sextortionist sentenced to 33 years for targeting 145 children
items.description: A Canadian man was sentenced to 33 years in prison after pleading guilty to targeting more than 145 children across the United States, some as young as 6 years old, in an eight-year-long sextortion scheme. [...]

items.title: BTMOB RAT Spreads Across Brazil, LatAm via MaaS Model
items.description: An advanced remote access Trojan is propagating online. Notably, it's delivered via an operator licensing model and features a no-code malware-development interface.

items.title: JINX-0164 Targets Cryptocurrency Firms with Fake Recruiter Lures and macOS Malware
items.description: A new campaign orchestrated by a previously undocumented threat actor has targeted cryptocurrency organizations with an aim to facilitate digital asset theft using recruitment-themed social engineering and bespoke macOS malware. "These campaigns leveraged sophisticated social engineering techniques, custom macOS malware, and deep targeting of CI/CD infrastructure," Wiz researchers Shira Ayal,

items.title: Nordic CISOs Handle Rising Cyber Threats Remarkably Well
items.description: Artificial intelligence notwithstanding, the vast majority of CISOs in northern Europe say they're facing no more serious cyberattacks than they did two years ago.

items.title: ISC Stormcast For Thursday, May 28th, 2026 https://isc.sans.edu/podcastdetail/9948, (Thu, May 28th)
items.description: (c) SANS Internet Storm Center. https://isc.sans.edu Creative Commons Attribution-Noncommercial 3.0 United States License.

items.title: Reconstructing an Akira Ransomware Kill Chain from Perimeter and Endpoint Logs, (Wed, May 27th)
items.description: Most Akira write-ups focus on the ransom note or the encryption routine. By the time those show up the interesting forensic work is over. The questions that matter to defenders sit earlier. How did they get in. When did they get domain admin. What did they touch before the binary fired. Those answers live in the days before impact. They sit in two log sources that almost never get joined. The perimeter firewall and the Windows event channel.

items.title: Ransomware Actors Show Up In Person to Steal Law Firm Data
items.description: The FBI warned that the extortion gang Silent Ransom Group is targeting law firms and social-engineering its way into servers and databases.

items.title: Romanian national sentenced to more than 4 years for hacking Oregon government systems
items.description: Dragomir was arrested in Romania in November 2024 and brought to the U.S. last year to face charges for hacking into the network belonging to Oregon’s Office of Emergency Management.

items.title: Latin American Cybercriminals Hoover Up Government Data
items.description: A purported leak exposing 5.8 million records of Uruguayan citizens is the latest incident where cybercriminals targeted government agencies to monetize citizen data.

items.title: AI-Assisted Exploit Development Outpaces Scanner Detection
items.description: Attackers are using AI to dramatically reduce the time they need to develop a working exploit for a CVE, according to new research.

items.title: Grandoreiro Malware and BTMOB RAT Campaigns Target Windows and Android Users
items.description: Latin America and Europe become the target of two banking trojan campaigns that are designed to infect Windows and Android devices with Grandoreiro and BTMOB malware, respectively. That's according to new findings from WatchGuard and ESET, which have observed the two malware families being used to single out companies in Spain, Portugal, and Mexico, as well as mobile users in Brazil. The

items.title: Malicious npm Package Stole Files From Claude AI User Directory via GitHub
items.description: Cybersecurity researchers have discovered a new malicious package on the npm registry that comes with information stealing capabilities. According to OX Security, the package, named "mouse5212-super-formatter," is designed to upload files from "/mnt/user-data," a dedicated directory used by Anthropic's Claude artificial intelligence (AI) tool to handle uploads and outputs in the background. The

items.title: 5 Steps to Managing Shadow AI Tools Without Slowing Down Employees
items.description: When an employee installs an AI writing assistant, connects a coding copilot to their IDE, or starts summarizing meetings with a new browser tool, they are doing exactly what a productive employee should do: finding faster ways to work. Across most organizations today, employees are running three to five AI tools on any given day. Most were never reviewed by IT. A significant portion connects

items.title: Cybersecurity Evolution: How We Went From Perimeter Defense to AI-Native Security
items.description: The cybersecurity industry of 2006 barely resembled today's billion-dollar behemoth. As part of Dark Reading's 20th anniversary celebration, we trace the industry's evolution through a technology lens.

items.title: GlassWorm Malware Takedown Disrupts Developer Supply Chain Attack Infrastructure
items.description: CrowdStrike, in partnership with Google and the Shadowserver Foundation, has announced the simultaneous disruption of all command-and-control (C2) channels associated with GlassWorm, a persistent software chain campaign targeting software developers through malicious packages and extensions. "Since at least early 2025, GlassWorm operators have systematically targeted software developers, a

items.description: (c) SANS Internet Storm Center. https://isc.sans.edu Creative Commons Attribution-Noncommercial 3.0 United States License.

items.title: CISA Announces Revised Town Hall Schedule to Engage with Stakeholders on Cyber Incident Reporting for Critical Infrastructure
ISC Stormcast For Tuesday, May 26th, 2026 https://isc.sans.edu/podcastdetail/9944, (Tue, May 26th)
items.description: (c) SANS Internet Storm Center. https://isc.sans.edu Creative Commons Attribution-Noncommercial 3.0 United States License.

items.title: Possible ACR Stealer From Page Impersonating Claude, (Tue, May 26th)
items.description: Introduction

items.title: Microsoft Access VBA, (Mon, May 25th)
items.description: Microsoft Access files (Microsoft Office&&#x23;x26;&#x23;39;s Database) can contain VBA code.

items.title: TeamPCP Supply Chain Campaign: Activity Through 2026-05-24, (Mon, May 25th)
items.description: TeamPCP now operates across three package ecosystems in parallel, it reached GitHub&&#x23;x26;&#x23;39;s own internal codebase, it trojanized an officially Microsoft-published Python SDK, and it appears to have open-sourced its own framework on GitHub.

items.title: Netherlands Seizes 800 Servers, Arrests 2 for Aiding Cyberattacks
items.description: Authorities in the Netherlands have arrested the co-owners of two related Internet hosting companies for operating IT infrastructure used by Russia to carry out cyberattacks, influence operations and disinformation campaigns inside the European Union. The two men were the focus of a 2025 KrebsOnSecurity story about how their hosting companies had assumed control over the technical infrastructure of Stark Industries Solutions, an Internet service provider sanctioned last year by the EU as a frequent staging ground for cyber mischief from Russia's intelligence agencies.

items.title: Wireshark 4.6.6 Released, (Sun, May 24th)
items.description: Wireshark release 4.6.6 fixes 1 vulnerability and 11 bugs.

items.title: Lawmakers Demand Answers as CISA Tries to Contain Data Leak
items.description: Lawmakers in both houses of Congress are demanding answers from the U.S. Cybersecurity & Infrastructure Security Agency (CISA) after KrebsOnSecurity reported this week that a CISA contractor intentionally published AWS GovCloud keys and a vast trove of other agency secrets on a public GitHub account. The inquiry comes as CISA is still struggling to contain the breach and invalidate the leaked credentials.

items.title: Alleged Kimwolf Botmaster ‘Dort’ Arrested, Charged in U.S. and Canada
items.description: Canadian authorities on Wednesday arrested a 23-year-old Ottawa man on suspicion of building and operating Kimwolf, a fast spreading Internet-of-Things botnet that enslaved millions of devices for use in a series of massive distributed denial-of-service (DDoS) attacks over the past six months. KrebsOnSecurity publicly named the suspect in February 2026 after the accused launched a volley of DDoS, doxing and swatting campaigns against this author and a security researcher. He now faces criminal hacking charges in both Canada and the United States.

items.title: CISA Enhances Known Exploited Vulnerabilities Catalog to Include New Nomination Form
CISA Admin Leaked AWS GovCloud Keys on Github
items.description: Until this past weekend, a contractor for the Cybersecurity & Infrastructure Security Agency (CISA) maintained a public GitHub repository that exposed credentials to several highly privileged AWS GovCloud accounts and a large number of internal CISA systems. Security experts said the public archive included files detailing how CISA builds, tests and deploys software internally, and that it represents one of the most egregious government data leaks in recent history.

items.title: Patch Tuesday, May 2026 Edition
items.description: Artificial intelligence platforms may be just as susceptible to social engineering as human beings, but they are proving remarkably good at finding security vulnerabilities in human-made computer code. That reality is on full display this month with some of the more widely-used software makers -- including Apple, Google, Microsoft, Mozilla and Oracle -- fixing near record volumes of security bugs, and/or quickening the tempo of their patch releases.

items.title: Canvas Breach Disrupts Schools & Colleges Nationwide
items.description: An ongoing data extortion attack targeting the widely-used education technology platform Canvas disrupted classes and coursework at school districts and universities across the United States today, after a cybercrime group defaced the service's login page with a ransom demand that threatened to leak data from 275 million students and faculty across nearly 9,000 educational institutions.

items.title: CISA Unveils New Initiative to Fortify America’s Critical Infrastructure
CISA, US and International Partners Release Guide to Secure Adoption of Agentic AI
Anti-DDoS Firm Heaped Attacks on Brazilian ISPs
items.description: A Brazilian tech firm that specializes in protecting networks from distributed denial-of-service (DDoS) attacks has been enabling a botnet responsible for an extended campaign of massive DDoS attacks against other network operators in Brazil, KrebsOnSecurity has learned. The firm's chief executive says the malicious activity resulted from a security breach and was likely the work of a competitor trying to tarnish his company's public image.

items.title: CISA and U.S. Government Partners Unveil Guide to Accelerate Zero Trust Adoption in Operational Technology
CISA, National Cyber Security Centre (NCSC) UK, and Global Partners Issue Advisory on Chinese Government-Linked Covert Cyber Networks
CISA Warns of FIRESTARTER Malware Targeting Cisco ASA including Firepower and Secure Firewall Products
‘Scattered Spider’ Member ‘Tylerb’ Pleads Guilty
items.description: A 24-year-old British national and senior member of the cybercrime group "Scattered Spider" has pleaded guilty to wire fraud conspiracy and aggravated identity theft. Tyler Robert Buchanan admitted his role in a series of text-message phishing attacks in the summer of 2022 that allowed the group to hack into at least a dozen major technology companies and steal tens of millions of dollars worth of cryptocurrency from investors.

items.title: Patch Tuesday, April 2026 Edition
items.description: Microsoft today pushed software updates to fix a staggering 167 security vulnerabilities in its Windows operating systems and related software, including a SharePoint Server zero-day and a publicly disclosed weakness in Windows Defender dubbed "BlueHammer." Separately, Google Chrome fixed its fourth zero-day of 2026, and an emergency update for Adobe Reader nixes an actively exploited flaw that can lead to remote code execution.

items.title: Russia Hacked Routers to Steal Microsoft Office Tokens
items.description: Hackers linked to Russia's military intelligence units are using known flaws in older Internet routers to mass harvest authentication tokens from Microsoft Office users, security experts warned today. The spying campaign allowed state-backed Russian hackers to quietly siphon authentication tokens from users on more than 18,000 networks without deploying any malicious software or code.

items.title: CISA Issues Updated RESURGE Malware Analysis Highlighting a Stealthy but Active Threat
Immediate Action Required: CISA Issues Emergency Directive to Secure Cisco SD-WAN Systems
CISA Announces New Town Halls to Engage with Stakeholders on Cyber Incident Reporting for Critical Infrastructure
Student Loan Breach Exposes 2.5M Records
items.description: 2.5 million people were affected, in a breach that could spell more trouble down the line.

items.title: Watering Hole Attacks Push ScanBox Keylogger
items.description: Researchers uncover a watering hole attack likely carried out by APT TA423, which attempts to plant the ScanBox JavaScript-based reconnaissance tool.

items.title: Tentacles of ‘0ktapus’ Threat Group Victimize 130 Firms
items.description: Over 130 companies tangled in sprawling phishing campaign that spoofed a multi-factor authentication system.

items.title: Ransomware Attacks are on the Rise
items.description: Lockbit is by far this summer’s most prolific ransomware group, trailed by two offshoots of the Conti group.

items.title: Cybercriminals Are Selling Access to Chinese Surveillance Cameras
items.description: Tens of thousands of cameras have failed to patch a critical, 11-month-old CVE, leaving thousands of organizations exposed.

items.title: Twitter Whistleblower Complaint: The TL;DR Version
items.description: Twitter is blasted for security and privacy lapses by the company’s former head of security who alleges the social media giant’s actions amount to a national security risk.

items.title: Firewall Bug Under Active Attack Triggers CISA Warning
items.description: CISA is warning that Palo Alto Networks’ PAN-OS is under active attack and needs to be patched ASAP.

items.title: Fake Reservation Links Prey on Weary Travelers
items.description: Fake travel reservations are exacting more pain from the travel weary, already dealing with the misery of canceled flights and overbooked hotels.

items.title: iPhone Users Urged to Update to Patch 2 Zero-Days
items.description: Separate fixes to macOS and iOS patch respective flaws in the kernel and WebKit that can allow threat actors to take over devices and are under attack.

items.title: Google Patches Chrome’s Fifth Zero-Day of the Year
items.description: An insufficient validation input flaw, one of 11 patched in an update this week, could allow for arbitrary code execution and is under active attack.
